title: Johnson & Johnson — Financials
html:
Module 3: Financial Architecture & Budget Intelligence

Governing Thought

The J&J account spans multiple active SOWs across 6 TAs — financial visibility requires triangulating Kimble project data, cluster management pipeline data, and the client-facing scorecard. Budget intelligence suggests J&J's total market research spend is $15M+ with preferred vendor pricing giving ZoomRx a ~50% professional fee discount. A new Principal must understand both how ZoomRx prices J&J work (which is structurally different from other accounts due to that discount) and where J&J is headed on budget philosophy (cost reduction through AI). Getting Kimble literacy on Day 1 is non-negotiable.

Where to Find Financial Data

Three systems hold the financial picture. None of them alone is sufficient.

System What It Holds Access Update Cadence
Kimble Revenue, project-level financials, effort tracking, invoicing Internal ZoomRx (web app) Real-time as PMs update
Cluster Management (OneDrive) Revenue/Wins/Pipeline by cluster & account; utilization OneDrive, Base 1 file (URI: 01PLAO6JD4M7EVDXOYNRH375466IS2IYLW) Weekly refresh
J&J Scorecard Dashboard Cross-brand portfolio scorecard with study-level detail SharePoint (.xlsx) Updated per wave/quarter

How they connect: Kimble gives you the ZoomRx-side truth — what we've invoiced, what's in progress, what's in the pipeline. Cluster Management shows the same data rolled up at the cluster level alongside other accounts, so you can see J&J's share of cluster revenue. The Scorecard Dashboard is client-facing — it's what J&J sees and uses to evaluate our performance across brands (e.g., SoV metrics, LTIP alignment).

Principal note: Start with Kimble for your first week. Pull the J&J project list, sort by revenue, and map each project to a brand/TA. That exercise alone will orient you faster than any briefing document.

J&J Market Research Budget Intelligence

What We Know

Data Point Source Confidence
J&J total MR budget: "over $15M" Heather Kulesik (rough estimate, AI Intel meeting) Low — likely understated
Amgen MR budget: $22M Same meeting, used as comparison Medium
Brand-level benchmark: XARELTO MR budget was $1M Heather's prior brand SAI role High (but dated)
NPS study alone is "multi-million" Heather Kulesik Medium

Interpretation: If Amgen runs $22M in market research and J&J's portfolio is broader, the $15M figure is almost certainly an undercount. Either Heather's visibility was partial (she may not see all TA budgets) or the figure excludes certain categories (e.g., NPS, global studies). A more realistic range is $15-25M across all TAs.

The AI Cost-Savings Agenda

J&J is actively exploring whether AI can "reduce the need for as much market research" (Heather's words). This is not hypothetical — it reflects a broader J&J enterprise push toward AI-enabled efficiency.

What this means for ZoomRx:

  • Expect increasing pressure to justify the ROI of every study
  • Proposals that include AI components (Dynamic Dialogues, AI-powered moderation) will have a structural advantage
  • Pure "data collection and reporting" SOWs are most vulnerable to compression
  • Consultative, insight-driven deliverables are harder to automate and therefore more defensible

Pricing Architecture

The Preferred Vendor Discount

J&J receives a ~50% professional fee discount through ZoomRx preferred vendor pricing. This is the single most important financial fact on the account — it means:

  1. Every project's professional fees are roughly half what we'd charge a non-preferred client
  2. Pass-through costs (fielding, incentives, translations) are not discounted
  3. Margin management is tighter than on other accounts; scope creep is more costly

TREMFYA GI Global Proposal — Pricing Reference (March 2026)

This is the most recent multi-market proposal and serves as a reliable pricing template.

Salesforce Effectiveness (SFE): $322,683 Total

Market Sample (N) Prof Fees Pass-Through Total
US 177 $70,415 $31,000 $101,415
UK 40 — — $25,596
Spain 40 — — $28,096
Italy 40 — — $28,096
Germany 40 — — $28,096
France 40 — — $28,096
Brazil 30 — — $30,596
Japan 30 — — $27,596
Australia 15 — — $25,096

Medical Engagement Effectiveness (MEE): $188,472 Total

Market Sample (N) Total
US — Already in progress under Medical Insights
UK 20 $21,934
Spain 20 $24,434
Italy 20 $24,434
Germany 20 $24,434
France 20 $24,434
Brazil 10 $24,434
Japan 10 $23,434
Australia 5 $20,934

Rx Data Integration (US Only): $57,500

Includes a zero-cost value add worth approximately $40K: one-time MSL impact analysis at account level. This is a strategic move — it demonstrates capability without charging, building the case for recurring Rx integration work.

Pricing Heuristics

From the TREMFYA data, you can extract rough per-market benchmarks:

Component US Ballpark Ex-US (Large Market) Ex-US (Small Market)
SFE per market $100K (N~175) $25-30K (N~40) $25K (N~15-30)
MEE per market Bundled separately $20-25K (N~20) $20-23K (N~5-10)
Rx Integration $55-60K N/A N/A

Principal note: When scoping a new J&J brand, use these as starting anchors. US SFE with N=150-200 will land around $100K. Adding 8 ex-US markets at ~$27K each adds ~$220K. Total global SFE: ~$320K. MEE runs roughly 60% of SFE cost.

Active Workstream Revenue Map

The J&J account runs 11+ concurrent workstream types. Each has a different revenue profile:

Workstream Revenue Type Cadence Typical SOW Structure
SFEA (Sales Rep Tracking) Recurring Monthly waves Annual SOW, invoiced per wave
OCE Tracking Recurring Monthly Annual SOW
MSL Tracking Recurring Quarterly Annual SOW
FRM Tracking Recurring Quarterly Annual SOW
KAM Tracking Recurring Quarterly Annual SOW
Ferma Congress Analytics Event-driven Per congress Per-event SOW or bundled annual
ATU (Awareness/Trial/Usage) Periodic Quarterly or semi-annual Annual SOW
Launch KPI Tracking Time-bound Monthly during launch window Project SOW (6-12 months)
Digital Tracking Recurring Monthly Annual SOW
Patient Experience (NPS) Recurring Multi-million annual Annual SOW
Qual Interviews / Message Labs Ad hoc Per request Project SOW

Revenue concentration risk: The SFEA and NPS workstreams likely account for a disproportionate share of total J&J revenue. Losing either would be material. The Scorecard Dashboard tracks study-level detail (e.g., Prostate Sales Rep Effectiveness, AKEEGA competitive deep-dives) — cross-reference it with Kimble to see revenue per study.

Financial Questions to Answer on Day 1

Pull these from Kimble in your first week:

Revenue & Pipeline

  • What is J&J's total trailing-12-month revenue to ZoomRx?
  • What are the top 5 J&J projects by revenue? Which brands do they belong to?
  • What is the current J&J pipeline (proposals submitted but not yet won)?
  • What J&J SOWs are expiring in the next 90 days and need renewal?

Profitability

  • What is the blended margin on J&J work after the 50% professional fee discount?
  • Which J&J projects are running over-effort (Kimble hours vs. budget)?
  • Are any projects at risk of scope creep without a change order?

Cluster Context

  • What share of your cluster's total revenue comes from J&J?
  • How does J&J's win rate compare to other accounts in the cluster?

Benchmarking

  • How does J&J's per-study pricing compare to non-preferred accounts running similar work?
  • What is the effective discount rate across project types (it may vary from the stated 50%)?

Watch Items

J&J AI Cost-Savings Agenda (HIGH)

J&J leadership is actively evaluating how AI can reduce market research spend. This is not a vague future concern — it was raised directly by Heather Kulesik. ZoomRx's AI capabilities (Dynamic Dialogues, AI moderation, automated reporting) are a defensive moat here, but only if we proactively demonstrate them. If J&J starts running AI-based research internally or through a competitor, our volume-based recurring work (especially standard SFEA waves) is at risk.

Action: Ensure every proposal includes at least one AI-enabled component. Position ZoomRx as the partner accelerating their AI agenda, not the vendor being replaced by it.

Budget Consolidation Risk (MEDIUM)

The $15M+ budget is spread across TAs with different budget holders. Any J&J reorganization that consolidates MR budgets under fewer decision-makers creates both risk (a single "no" kills more revenue) and opportunity (a single "yes" unlocks more). Monitor for signs of centralized procurement.

Preferred Vendor Pricing Review (MEDIUM)

The ~50% discount is a relationship anchor but also a margin constraint. If J&J's procurement team benchmarks ZoomRx against cheaper AI-native vendors, they may push for even steeper discounts. Conversely, if the discount was set years ago, there may be room to renegotiate specific components (e.g., pass-through markups, value-add pricing for consulting hours).

NPS Study Scale (LOW but watch)

The NPS study is described as "multi-million" — this likely makes it one of ZoomRx's largest single-project SOWs across all accounts. Any change to its scope, frequency, or vendor assignment would be a significant revenue event. Know who owns the NPS relationship on the J&J side and ensure satisfaction is actively managed.

Back to Accounts Hub | Home
